title: CALL FOR APPLICATIONS TO HOST INTERNATIONAL DATA WEEK 2029
content:
International Data Week (IDW) is organised jointly by three leading global data organisations:

CODATA advances global collaboration to improve the availability, usability, and stewardship of research data, promoting Open Science across disciplines.

Research Data Alliance (RDA) develops and implements community-driven infrastructure and best practices to enable open sharing and reuse of data worldwide.

World Data System (WDS) supports trusted scientific data repositories and services, advocating for accessible, high-quality, and reproducible research data.

TOGETHER, THESE FOUNDING ORGANISATIONS ARE INVITING PROPOSALS TO HOST THE SEVENTH EDITION OF IDW IN SEPTEMBER/OCTOBER/NOVEMBER 2029.

The primary objective of the IDW conference is to bring together a large, representative portion of the global data community. Previous editions have been highly successful in fostering collaboration, innovation, and impact. As the global landscape evolves, the founding organisations seek local partners and venues that can help convey the excitement, mission, and urgency of these times in data science.

Five editions have taken place to date, and one is scheduled and in preparation:
snippet: International Data Week (IDW) is organised jointly by three leading global data organisations:
creator: Asha
sourceDescription: Committee on Data of the ISC

title: CDIF4EOSC is Underway!
content:
The CDIF4EOSC project, coordinated by CODATA, started on 1 June 2026!

The distinct approach of the CDIF4EOSC project (CDIF4EOSC: Developing and implementing the Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework for EOSC) is to build on the Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework (CDIF). The project will extend the CDIF recommendations, adding profiles and guidelines, and use case based examples, to form a comprehensive and actionable playbook for FAIR Integration in the European Open Science Cloud (EOSC) and beyond.

This is a remarkable opportunity to extend and refine CDIF, and to furnish EOSC with a comprehensive and implementable guide to interoperability!

Going beyond the original FAIR principles, CDIF4EOSC will develop a FAIR-by-design approach for creating FAIR digital objects. The overarching objective of the project is to enable FAIR integration by developing the CDIF4EOSC Playbook and situating this at the heart of EOSC practices, and fully integrated in the EOSC Federation.
snippet: The CDIF4EOSC project, coordinated by CODATA, started on 1 June 2026!
creator: Asha
sourceDescription: Committee on Data of the ISC
